reasoning:
The question asks whether the CAS decision reallocating the Olympic bronze medal in women's floor exercise from Jordan Chiles to Ana Barbosu will be overturned before Jan 1, 2026. This would require the Swiss Federal Supreme Court (SFT) to issue an overturn ruling. Historically, the SFT overturns only about ~1% or less of CAS decisions, typically restricting its review to very narrow procedural grounds (e.g. conflicts of interest, right to be heard) rather than the merits. 

Jordan Chiles officially filed appeals with the SFT in September 2024. A November 2025 Sports Litigation Update by law firm Quinn Emanuel states that as of October 2025, the SFT has not yet issued a decision on either the setting-aside application or the request for revision, and no timeline has been established for when rulings will be rendered. Given that SFT decisions often take extensive time, no ruling has been made as of late 2025, and there are only a few weeks remaining until the January 1, 2026 cutoff, it is exceedingly unlikely that a ruling will be issued and that it will be a successful overturn. The community prediction (0.01) properly gauges this, so I estimate the probability of this resolving YES by the deadline at the minimum allowable threshold of 0.02.
